kept.text:
📍$CRWV （CoreWeave）
時価総額は約600億ドル規模

AI向けクラウドインフラ企業。

NVIDIA GPUを大量に使ったAIデータセンターを運営し、AIモデルの学習・推論に必要な計算能力を貸し出す会社。

✅ AIクラウド
✅ GPUインフラ
✅ データセンター
✅ 生成AI向け計算資源
✅ 大規模AI学習・推論

CoreWeaveは「AIネイティブなクラウド」を掲げていて、通常のクラウドというより、AIワークロード向けに特化したGPUクラウド企業という見方が近い。

AI相場は半導体だけではなく、
そのGPUを動かすクラウド・電力・冷却・データセンターまで広がっている。

$CRWVはその流れの代表格のひとつ。

kept.text:
Is AI the same as the dot-com bubble?

Are we going to see another global crash and economy reset?

Let's see what numbers say 👇

➤ NVIDIA is valued at $4.3 trillion, stock up 1300% since 2022.

➤ OpenAI raised $110 billion in a single round at a $730 billion valuation, the largest private funding round in history.

➤ AI startups attracted $258 billion in 2025 alone, that's 61% of ALL global VC money flowing into one sector.

➤ The top 5 companies now hold 30% of the entire S&P 500, the greatest concentration in half a century.

🚨 54% of fund managers openly call AI stocks a bubble.

Now here's where it gets scary:

Microsoft funds OpenAI:
OpenAI buys compute from CoreWeave, CoreWeave leases GPUs from NVIDIA, financed again by Microsoft and Oracle. The same dollar recycled across balance sheets, not organic growth, a circular liquidity loop.

And all of this is happening while the US national debt just crossed $39 trillion.

Debt-to-GDP hit 100% for the first time since World War II, and the US is now spending more on interest payments ($1 trillion in 2026) than it spends on national defense.

AI-related investment currently accounts for half of US GDP growth. If AI spending pulls back, GDP growth slows, debt-to-GDP gets worse, and the entire system starts compounding against itself.

The dot-com bubble didn't burst because the internet failed, it burst because valuations assumed it would replace gravity.

Nasdaq lost 78% before we got Amazon and Google out the other side.

AI is real, the technology is transformative, but the economy sitting underneath it is standing on $39 trillion of debt with a trillion dollar interest bill.

What side are you on.

kept.text:
$NBIS UPDATE:  Nebius publicly disclosed receiving 40% pre payment in Microsoft deal 🔥🔥🔥

Nebius 20-F filing reveals:

(1).  Microsoft deal now discloses $6.9B upfront prepayment (~40% of $17.4B TCV) for the first time.  This crushes the typical 15-25% at $IREN and $CRWV

(2).  Nebius 2nd GPU tranche for Microsoft was delivered ON TIME in February

profile_bio.description: Screenwriter, Member Writers Guild of America West, M.F.A. USC Cinema, Juris Doctor, Tech Investor * Not a Financial Advisor (Always Do Your Own Research)

quoted_tweet.text:
$nbis Overlooked amidst recent press releases, co. dropped their 20-F late last week. Some news, confirmatory nuggets, & potential read-through for upcoming earnings:

1. Microsoft Deal: 

--Upfront prepayment pegged @ ~40% of TCV ($6.95B on $17.39B contact value), which MStanley sees as significantly “higher than expected” and well above crwv’s referenced 15-25% prepayments

--Successful delivery of 2nd tranche from Vineland detailed against a 9 tranche delivery schedule; corroboration MS has contracted service-level credit compensation & termination rights

2. DC Strategy & Dev List

  --Commentary on secured contracts across Europe, Middle East & US

3. Potential Benefits from Higher Pricing

--Disclosure of a RPO @ $21.3B (pre-2nd Meta deal), plus a declaration that (outside Meta/Microsoft) “most of our customer engagements to date have relatively short term” implies possible read-through benefit from positive pricing trends

--In a note earlier this month, Mstanley marked Nebius's exposure to spot market pricing  at its ~550M arr base, suggesting it could lead to near term “outperformance"
